description: Lock Early Scientific Conferences to Royal Society meeting, lecture, correspondence, demonstration, discussion, and publication culture, not journal peer-review governance.

checks.reason: Royal Society meetings require the scientific-society institutional framework.
The node is scoped to society-based meeting culture.
The first Royal Society meeting followed a Gresham College astronomy lecture.
Public lecture culture is the immediate historical setting for the first meeting.
Royal Society meetings gathered information through correspondence and discussed results.
Correspondence networks are shared information infrastructure for meetings and publication.
Peer-review practices should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to journal refereeing governance.
The node remains anchored to Royal Society meeting publication/proceedings culture around Philosophical Transactions.
